reasoning.A:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: I’m sure my own Q1 answer is correct, and recent puzzles for Q2–Q4 have all been simple arithmetic that everyone has consistently solved correctly. In the last three games, someone (usually B or C) has volunteered in Round 1 every time, so the chance that *no one* volunteers now—and we face a random kill—is small.  

If I volunteer, my only way to die is if any of Q2–Q4 is wrong, which is still a direct personal death risk. If I pass, I mainly risk random death only in the unlikely event that nobody volunteers (25% × P(no volunteer)), which is probably lower than the risk of a wrong capsule. Verifying would just cost a coin without improving my immediate survival odds this round.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle 44 ÷ 4 is trivial and I’m fully confident in my answer, and past games have shown that everyone almost always solves these basic arithmetic questions correctly—every verification so far has agreed with the existing capsules, and every volunteer using the simple Q1-A/Q2-B/Q3-C/Q4-D set has escaped successfully. If I don’t volunteer, there’s a real chance nobody does in Round 1 and we face a random elimination, giving me a 1-in-4 death risk regardless of how careful I was. Verifying first would cost a coin and still expose me to that same random-death risk this round. Volunteering now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D maximizes my survival odds while keeping all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own answer for Q3 (50 ÷ 10) is correct. All puzzles so far have been simple arithmetic, and aside from one early mistake by D, every capsule across recent games has been correct on the first try, even without prior verification. Right now there are no conflicting capsules, so each of the others (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q4-D) is very likely right.

If I don’t volunteer, I still face the risk of random elimination this round if everyone waits—verifying doesn’t reduce that risk at all, it only costs me a coin. Given the strong accuracy track record and the simplicity of the tasks, the probability that this full password is correct is high enough that volunteering with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my chance of both surviving and keeping all 4 coins.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
